# wedeo

> AI-generated Rust rewrite of FFmpeg.

wedeo translates FFmpeg's C source into idiomatic Rust, verified bit-for-bit
against FFmpeg's FATE test suite. 18 crates, ~52K lines of Rust.

## Project structure

- CLAUDE.md: Full project context, architecture, conventions, debugging (READ THIS FIRST)
- H264.md: H.264 decoder internals, module map, conformance status
- CONTRIBUTING.md: Contribution guidelines, what we accept/reject
- DIVERGENCES.md: Known behavioral differences vs FFmpeg

## Key files for AI agents

- CLAUDE.md — canonical rules, build commands, debugging procedures
- codecs/wedeo-codec-h264/src/ — H.264 decoder (30K lines, 25 modules)
- codecs/wedeo-codec-vp9/src/ — VP9 decoder (keyframe-only, 12 modules)
- scripts/suite_runner.py — unified conformance runner
- scripts/conformance_full.py — FATE conformance report
- tests/fate/ — Rust FATE test harness
- FFmpeg/ — reference C source (git submodule, n8.1)

## Build and verify

- cargo clippy && cargo fmt --check — must pass before any PR
- cargo nextest run — 462 tests
- python3 scripts/suite_runner.py --suite all --format yuv420p — full conformance

## Current focus

H.264 decoder: 79/79 progressive conformance BITEXACT, expanding to MBAFF
interlaced and FRext profiles. Performance optimization via aarch64 NEON.
VP9 decoder: keyframe-only native Rust decoder with IVF demuxer. Inter
prediction deferred.
Video player (wedeo-play): GPU-accelerated via wgpu+winit, ffplay-style
A/V sync with pts_drift audio clock, 24fps 0-drop 1080p playback.

## Philosophy

"Make it work, make it right, make it fast." Correctness first. Reproduce
FFmpeg bugs when needed for behavioral parity. Copy FFmpeg comments verbatim.
